summary: No credible evidence shows herbal detox teas prevent or treat COVID-19.
key_points: COVID-19 prevention relies on vaccination and proven measures
Herbal teas are not protective or curative

topic: Handwashing reduces spread of respiratory infections
summary: Multiple studies support hand hygiene reducing transmission of respiratory viruses.
key_points: Public health agencies recommend regular handwashing

topic: Antibiotics treat viral infections such as colds
summary: Colds are caused by viruses and do not respond to antibiotics; misuse increases resistance.
Strong evidence shows HPV vaccines prevent cervical and other HPV-related cancers.
key_points: Vaccination before exposure is most effective
Population studies show reduced precancerous lesions

topic: Vitamin C prevents common colds in the general population
summary: Routine vitamin C supplementation does not reliably prevent colds; slight benefit in duration and in extreme physical stress.
key_points: Potential modest effect in special groups
citations: Cochrane review on vitamin C and colds

topic: Face masks reduce spread of respiratory viruses
summary: Mask use can reduce emission and inhalation of respiratory droplets/aerosols.
key_points: Effect depends on fit and compliance

topic: Regular physical activity reduces risk of type 2 diabetes
summary: Consistent evidence links regular exercise with lower incidence of type 2 diabetes.

topic: Megadoses of vitamin D prevent all infections
summary: No evidence supports that very high doses of vitamin D prevent all infections; excess intake can be harmful.
key_points: Focus on adequate, not excessive, intake
